2606.18237.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
2606.18237.paper_summary: Reproducing research results from papers and released code is central to scientific progress. Existing works have introduced benchmarks to evaluate whether LLM agents can assist with reproducibility, but they are difficult to scale due to their reliance on substantial manual effort for data curation and evaluation. We introduce ReproRepo, a scalable framework for reproducibility evaluation that leverages human-raised GitHub issues as naturally occurring supervision on realistic reproduction blockers. We instantiate ReproRepo on 1,149 recent machine learning papers from major conferences and evaluate four frontier model-agent configurations. Our results show that LLM agents, even without executing code, can identify many real-world reproducibility problems from paper-repository pairs: the best agent in our study, namely Codex with GPT-5.5, surfaces at least one semantically related human-reported blocker for ~90% of papers in the study. Further analysis shows that agents are particularly effective for surfacing visible failures and identifying the right semantic region, but may still be insufficient in exact localization. ReproRepo can serve as a reusable, scalable framework for future evaluations of LLM agents on real-world reproducibility auditing. Our code is released at https://github.com/LithiumDA/ReproRepo.
2606.18237.paper_summary_zh: 重現研究結果的論文和釋出的代碼是科學進步的核心。現有的研究已經引入基準來評估 LLM 代理是否能協助實現可重現性，但由於依賴大量的手動數據整理和評估工作，這些基準難以擴展。我們介紹了 ReproRepo，一個可擴展的可重現性評估框架，利用人為提出的 GitHub 問題作為對現實重現障礙的自然監督。我們在來自主要會議的 1,149 篇近期機器學習論文上實現了 ReproRepo，並評估了四種前沿模型-代理配置。我們的結果顯示，即使不執行代碼，LLM 代理也能從論文-庫對中識別出許多現實世界的可重現性問題：在我們的研究中，最佳代理，即 Codex with GPT-5.5，能夠為約 90% 的論文提出至少一個語義相關的人為報告障礙。進一步的分析顯示，代理在揭示明顯的失敗和識別正確的語義區域方面特別有效，但在精確定位上可能仍然不足。ReproRepo 可以作為未來對 LLM 代理在現實世界可重現性審核中進行評估的可重用、可擴展框架。我們的代碼已在 https://github.com/LithiumDA/ReproRepo 釋出。
2606.18237.author: Shanda Li et.al.
2606.18237.authors: Shanda Li, Qiuhong Anna Wei, Jingwu Tang, Valerie Chen, Nihar B Shah, Tim Dettmers, Yiming Yang, Ameet Talwalkar
